D7 Print L5 Evaluate Reference

HP MultiJet Fusion in Colour

spread a layer of nylon powder, jet black fusing agent where the part is, jet CMYK colour on top, sweep an infrared lamp — that voxel is now fused and dyed

MultiJet Fusion builds each layer in three passes over a powder bed: a spreader bar lays a thin film of nylon powder; a carriage jets a dark fusing agent only where the part will be; CMYK colourant follows; then an infrared lamp sweeps and the agent's heat absorption fuses just the marked powder. Stack hundreds of these layers and a coloured solid emerges from the bed.

Slide the build progress and watch the part rise from the powder bed, one coloured layer at a time. The process panel shows the carriage in the middle of its pass — spreader, fusing-agent jet, colour jet, infrared lamp — over the powder.
